title: What You Can Expect After You File an EEOC Charge
description: EEOC page describing the post-filing workflow for a private-sector discrimination charge: employer notification within 10 days, optional mediation (often under 3 months), investigation (averaging ~10 months), respondent position-statement process, and possible Notice of Right to Sue — plus rules for amending a charge and prerequisites before filing suit.
notes: Private-sector charges only (federal employees: see sub-08-eeoc-federal-sector). Employer notice within 10 days; respondent position-statement requested within 30 days; EEOC must be allowed 180 days before a Title VII/ADA Right-to-Sue request (60 days for ADEA suits; EPA suits within 2 years of last discriminatory paycheck).
